topic: excess nitrogen and vine disease risk

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Excess nitrogen stimulates vigorous shoot and leaf growth.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: The resulting dense canopy shades bunches, restricts airflow, and retains humidity around the fruit.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Ripening can be delayed and the humid canopy becomes more susceptible to fungal disease.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Ripening can be delayed and the humid canopy becomes more susceptible to fungal disease.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
